title: I Belong In Austin Report
text:
Portal Applications
Since opening our portal El Buen Samaritano has received 43,865 applications for assistance, of which 24,475 are estimated to be unique individuals. In some cases people have applied for assistance in multiple months, or multiple times within a month.






NoteMore On Determining the Number of Unique/Repeat Applicants





When people apply more than once, they might use different phone numbers or different versions of an address (N. Long Street / North Long St). To estimate the number of unique applicants, we assigned all applications a unique ID based on a non case-sensitive concatenation of first name, last name, and date of birth. The number of distinct unique IDs gives us our estimate of how many unique people applied, and the number of times each unique ID appears shows us how many times each of those individuals applied and when.



Based on each unique individual’s reported income and attestation that they live within the Austin full purpose jurisdiction at their most recent application, we believe that 15,523 total portal applicants were eligible for IBIA services based on their reported income and attestation of residence.






NoteMore On Determining Eligibility





When portal applications are processed for the random draw, an additional check is made comparing their reported address to the Austin full purpose jurisdiction in addition to the self-report yes/no question asked on the application form. This reduces the chance of ineligible clients being slected for contact. After clients are randomly selected for contact by El Buen’s community health navigators, our staff reach out to confirm and document each household’s income and geographic eligiblity.

title: I Belong In Austin Report
text:
Households Assisted
3,188 unique households have been assisted through IBIA, and the number of household members who have benefited total 8,870.
Of the above, 2,136 unique households came through El Buen and our IBIA Portal, totaling 6,558 household members.






WarningUnassisted Portal Applicants





El Buen has assited 1,221 unique households since opening the portal in the late fall of 2024. Based on the number of portal applicants who are suspected to meet eligibilty criteria, 14,302 or 92.13% of all eligible households have remained unassisted.

title: IBIA Program Data
text:
3,188 unique households have been assisted through IBIA, and the number of household members who have benefited total 8,870.



Of the above, 2,136 unique household came through El Buen and our IBIA Portal, totaling 6,558 household members.

title: El Buen Portal Data
text:
Note



The charts below show data for the El Buen IBIA Portal by month, per quarter, for the fiscal years the portal has been operative:

For each month, the first two columns combined (dark blue & orange) are the total number of applications per month, broken out by new (dark blue) and repeat (orange) applicants.
The third column (green) shows the subcount of applications meeting the historical eligibility criteria of address and income.
The fourth column (light blue) shows the subcount of applications reporting an eviction notice.
The fifth/final (purple) column shows the subcount of applications meeting the recently updated eligibility requirements for IBIA: (1) applicant’s reported location, (2) applicant’s reported income, and (3) applicant’s reported eviction status.

title: El Buen Portal Data
text: The total number of unique applicants who have applied through the El Buen IBIA Portal and report living within the Austin full purpose jurisdiction and falling at or below 80% AMI is 15,523.

title: I Belong In Austin Report
text:
Details about Applications

Income Brackets
Of applications received through the El Buen IBIA Portal:

4,421 reported “Zero Income.”
11,016 fall into the “Very Low” (30% or Below AMI) income bracket per the information reported by clients.
7 reported being in the “Low” (50% or Below AMI) income bracket.







NoteMore on Determing Income Brackets





Due to the nature of self-reported information, errors can occur. The above numbers are excluding cases where a form was filled out indicating more than 15 members in a single household (or zero member households), or where any null values occurred since in both cases AMI cannot be calculated to determine an income bracket. The numbers above indicate the totals of those who responded with valid information.





Eviction Notices

Portal Applicants
9,861 unique portal applicants (40.29%) reported having received an eviction notice. (Each applicant is only counted once. For those who applied multiple times, they are counted once if they reported having an eviction notice in any one or more of their applications.)


People Served
1,860 (58.34%) of individuals who recieved assistance through IBIA (including El Buen and all partners) reported having an active eviction during an enrollment.
El Buen served 45% of the above total, helping 845 unique clients who came directly to us with an eviction notice.

title: I Belong In Austin Report
section: Change In Volume of Applications By Year
text:
Change In Volume of Applications By Year

Applications in 2024

In 2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 9,362 total applications after it first opened, operating for under a quarter of the last part of the year (starting in November). If this volume had been constant per quarter before the portal opened, it is possible that we could have had 37,448 total applications in 2024.
In 2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 8,088 estimated unique applications after it first opened, operating for under a quarter of the last part of the year (starting in November). If this volume had constant per quarter before the portal opened, it is possible that we could have had 32,352 unique applications in 2024.
(86.39% of applications submitted in 2024 were from unique individuals).



Applications in 2025

In 2025 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 29,762 total applications.
In 2025 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 17,730 estimated unique applications.
(59.57% of applications submitted in 2025 were from unique individuals).



Applications in 2026

In 2026 El Buen’s IBIA portal has received 4,741 total applications since January, even with the additional eviction requirements for entry that have recently been added. If this volume remains per quarter and IBIA continues to operate, it is possible that we could have 18,964 total applications by the end of December 2026, even with the additional eviction requirements.
In 2026 El Buen’s IBIA portal has received 3,734 estimated unique applications since January, even with the additional eviction requirements for entry that have recently been added. If this volume remains per quarter and IBIA continues to operate, it is possible that we could have 14,936 unique applications by the end of December 2026, even with the additional eviction requirements.
(78.76% of applications submitted in 2026 so far have been from unique individuals).

title: I Belong In Austin Report
section: Change In Volume of Portal Applications By Fiscal Year
text:
Change In Volume of Portal Applications By Fiscal Year

Applications in FY24

In the fiscal year of 2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 32,428 total applications after it first opened (starting in November 2024).
In FY2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 19,644 estimated unique applications.
(60.58% of applications submitted in FY24 were from unique individuals.)







Applications in FY25

In FY2025 El Buen’s IBIA portal has received 11,437 total applications.
In FY2025 El Buen’s IBIA portal has received 4,831 estimated unique applications.
(42.24% of applications submitted in FY2025 have been from unique individuals).
